behaviors.description: Branch staleness detection correctly identifies branches with commits not in main, older than the configured age threshold, and no open PR.
behaviors.justification: This behavior is mainly cross-module state handoff (git-diff logic + age-threshold logic + PR-presence logic). It is not explicitly requiring live repo/network/subprocess contracts, and it has no AI dependence. Rejected System because the behavior text does not require live infrastructure or explicit external contract/round-trip verification.
behaviors.test_description: Run detector with integrated in-process collaborators: commit-state provider, branch-age calculator, and PR-state provider. Verify combined decision is stale only when all three predicates are true, and flips when any predicate is false.

behaviors.description: Session-start setup emits a stale-work warning with actionable branch context when the current branch is stale.
behaviors.justification: Failure risk is wiring/orchestration between session bootstrap, stale-state computation, and warning rendering payload. No explicit requirement for real OS/network/subprocess behavior, and no AI output dependence. Rejected System because behavior text asks for emitted warning content, not live external contract checks.
behaviors.test_description: Execute session-start pipeline with a stale branch state fixture from the real detector interface and assert one warning event/message is produced with required actionable fields (branch name, age, commit/PR context).

behaviors.description: A local status-classifier function maps computed branch state to labels (`clean`, `stale-no-pr`) deterministically.
behaviors.justification: Single local mapping function with deterministic input/output and no I/O boundaries. Higher-level checks add no new reality signal for this specific behavior.
behaviors.test_description: Table-driven unit test over branch-state inputs asserting exact label outputs and deterministic repeatability.

behaviors.description: `/kaizen-wip` against live repo and PR state distinguishes "clean" from "has commits but no PR (STALE)" in rendered output.
behaviors.justification: Behavior explicitly requires live repo + live PR state and externally rendered output. This hits real infrastructure and external contract/round-trip observation, so unit/integration fakes can hide failures. Rejected Agentic/Workflow because no model output quality or multi-step agentic chain is involved.
behaviors.test_description: Run `/kaizen-wip` in a real repository context with real PR backend state for two scenarios (clean vs unPRed stale). Assert rendered output text/status matches expected classification in each scenario.

behaviors.description: Branches with open or merged PRs are not flagged as stale-no-PR false positives.
behaviors.justification: This is decision correctness across branch-state + PR-status modules (open/merged should suppress stale-no-pr). Text does not explicitly require live API contract checks. Rejected System because no explicit live external response-shape/round-trip requirement is stated.
behaviors.test_description: Integrate staleness detector with PR-status provider interface and verify open and merged PR statuses both prevent stale-no-pr classification across relevant branch-age/commit combinations.

behaviors.description: Staleness detection remains correct across rebases or squash merges where commit hashes change but branch state should no longer be classified as unPRed stale work.
behaviors.justification: Rebase/squash semantics are git-history behaviors that commonly depend on real VCS/subprocess behavior and can be misrepresented by in-process fakes. The behavior requires observing post-operation branch classification after real history mutation (round-trip readback). Rejected Integration because synthetic commit graphs may miss real git edge cases tied to rewritten hashes.
behaviors.test_description: In a real git repo, create stale-like history, then perform actual rebase/squash-merge operations that rewrite commit hashes. Re-run staleness detection and assert branch is no longer classified as unPRed stale when logical work is already represented upstream.
